expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
expectations.evidence: No complexity/split advisory found. No mention of 'complexity', 'split', 'advisory', or 'benefit from splitting' in the review.

expectations.text: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 (Critical): 'Breaking Change: New method on IUserService without default implementation -- binary breaking change'. Issue 5 (High): 'IUserPresentationFactory new method without default implementation... adding members without defaults is a breaking change.'

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: Issue 10 flags 'Trailing blank lines added to UserService.cs' as a Low severity finding: 'Two blank lines are added before the #endregion at the end of the file. This is a minor whitespace issue.' Issue 13 flags 'Missing blank line before @customElement in modal element' as Low. Both are purely cosmetic formatting issues.

expectations.text: All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
expectations.evidence: All findings reference code from the diff or files touched by the PR. The notification localization finding (Issue 8) notes the pre-existing pattern but flags it for the new code. The hand-edited gen files are part of the diff.

expectations.text: Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach -- not just absence of bugs
expectations.evidence: The 'Observations (Non-blocking)' section includes: 'Architectural note on the workspace naming', 'Authorization pattern is consistent', 'The SectionUserNoPermissionCondition is well-designed'. These assess architecture and patterns.

expectations.text: Review does NOT flag UpdateCurrentUserAsync as missing UserSavingNotification/UserSavedNotification -- the sibling UpdateAsync also does not publish these notifications, so flagging their absence would be a false positive
expectations.evidence: The word 'notification' appears only in context of UI notifications (error display, localization of 'Avatar uploaded' messages), not about domain event notifications like UserSavingNotification/UserSavedNotification.

eval_feedback.overall: Good review with correct breaking change detection and feature assessment. Falls short on complexity advisory (absent) and includes cosmetic nitpicks (trailing blank lines, missing blank line before decorator).
